name: Archaeological complex of Mulva-Munigua — circular from Villanueva del Río y Minas
name_original: Conjunto arqueológico de Mulva-Munigua — circular desde Villanueva del Río y Minas
region_intro: Hiking around Villanueva Del Río Y Minas offers diverse landscapes at the foothills of the Sierra Morena and the edge of the Sierra Norte de Sevilla Natural Park. The region features varied topography, including mountainous terrain with olive groves, pasture lands, and evergreen oaks to the north, and plains to the south. The Rivera del Huéznar river traverses the territory, contributing to its natural features. Notable natural attractions include the Paraje Natural de los Arenales del Parroso and Lago Azul, an artificial lake formed in an abandoned open-pit mine. Best hiking trails around Villanueva Del Río Y Minas The most popular hiking route is Archaeological complex of Mulva-Munigua — circular from Villanueva del Río y Minas, a 10.6 miles (17.0 km) trail that takes 4 hours 37 minutes to complete. This moderate route leads to the ancient Roman archaeological site of Mulva-Munigua. Another top favourite among local hikers is Pozo 5 Mine and Lago Azul Trail, a moderate 4.9 miles (7.8 km) path. This circular trail explores historic mine sites, including the well-preserved Pozo 5, and the scenic Lago Azul. Local hikers also love the Lago Azul – Munigua Railway Line loop from Villanueva del Río y Minas, a 10.4 miles (16.7 km) trail leading through varied terrain including the Blue Lake and the old Munigua railway line, often completed in about 4 hours 30 minutes. Hiking around Villanueva Del Río Y Minas is defined by mountainous terrain, river valleys, and historical mining landscapes, offering a mix of natural beauty and cultural exploration. The network provides options for various ability levels, from easy walks to moderate excursions. The routes in Villanueva Del Río Y Minas are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.2 stars from over 10 reviews. More than 100 hikers have used komoot to explore Villanueva Del Río Y Minas's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What types of landscapes can I expect to see while hiking in Villanueva Del Río Y Minas?
A: The region offers a diverse topography, ranging from mountainous terrain adorned with olive groves, pasture lands, and evergreen oaks in the north, to expansive plains in the south. The Rivera del Huéznar river also diagonally traverses the territory, adding scenic river valleys to the mix. You'll encounter a unique blend of natural beauty and historical mining landscapes.

Q: Are there any easy hiking trails suitable for beginners or families?
A: Yes, Villanueva Del Río Y Minas offers several easy routes. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14621459" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Munigua, South necropolis loop&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 4.8 km trail that takes approximately 1 hour 17 minutes to complete, perfect for a relaxed outing.

Q: What are some notable natural attractions or landmarks to look out for on the trails?
A: Hikers can explore several unique natural and historical sites. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2676625" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lago Azul&lt;/a&gt; (Blue Lake), an artificial lake formed in an abandoned open-pit mine, is a visually striking feature. You can also visit the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4782188" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Archaeological Site of Mulva-Munigua&lt;/a&gt;, which includes a sanctuary, thermal baths, and a necropolis, offering a glimpse into the region's Roman past.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes available?
A: Yes, many of the trails in Villanueva Del Río Y Minas are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. A great example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1010093949/archaeological-complex-of-mulva-munigua-circular-from-villanueva-del-rio-y-minas" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Archaeological complex of Mulva-Munigua — circular from Villanueva del Río y Minas&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 17.0 km route, or the shorter &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1010099806/pozo-5-mine-and-lago-azul-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pozo 5 Mine and Lago Azul Trail&lt;/a&gt;, which is 7.8 km.

Q: Can I hike with my dog in Villanueva Del Río Y Minas?
A: Generally, the trails in Villanueva Del Río Y Minas are suitable for dogs, especially those that traverse natural landscapes like olive groves and river paths. However, it's always recommended to keep your dog on a leash, especially near archaeological sites or in areas where livestock may be present. Always carry water for your pet.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in this region?
A: The region is ideal for hiking during spring and autumn when temperatures are milder. Summers can be very hot, making early morning or late afternoon hikes preferable. Winter offers cooler temperatures, which can be pleasant, but some paths might be muddy after rain.

Q: Are there any trails that explore the region's mining history?
A: Absolutely. The area has a rich mining heritage. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1010099806/pozo-5-mine-and-lago-azul-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pozo 5 Mine and Lago Azul Trail&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 7.8 km circular route that specifically explores historic mine sites, including the well-preserved Pozo 5, offering a fascinating insight into the past.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Villanueva Del Río Y Minas?
A: The trails in Villanueva Del Río Y Minas are highly regarded by the komoot community, with an average rating of 4.2 stars from over 10 reviews. Hikers often praise the diverse terrain, the blend of natural beauty with historical elements, and the well-maintained paths that offer a mix of easy and moderate excursions.

Q: Are there any challenging hikes for experienced trekkers?
A: While most routes are moderate, the longer trails offer a good challenge due to their distance and varied terrain. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31682930" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Munigua Railway Line – Huéznar Reservoir loop&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 17.0 km route with significant elevation changes, providing a rewarding experience for those seeking a longer trek.

Q: Is there public transport access to the hiking trailheads?
A: Villanueva Del Río Y Minas is accessible by public transport, primarily bus services connecting it to Seville. While some trailheads might be directly accessible from the town, it's advisable to check local bus schedules and route maps in advance to plan your access to specific starting points.

Q: Are there opportunities for wild swimming or water activities near the hiking trails?
A: The Rivera del Huéznar river and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2676623" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Huéznar Reservoir&lt;/a&gt; offer opportunities for water-related activities. The Parque Fluvial Ribera del Hueznar within the urban area provides recreational areas, and while specific wild swimming spots might require local knowledge, the presence of these water bodies suggests possibilities for cooling off after a hike.

Q: How many hiking routes are available in Villanueva Del Río Y Minas?
A: There are over 10 hiking routes available in Villanueva Del Río Y Minas, catering to various fitness levels. These include 2 easy routes and 9 moderate routes, ensuring there's something for everyone.
tips: Structure of the viaduct over the Ribera del Huéznar, located at KP 194+562 of the Mérida-Sevilla railway, known as the “Iron Bridge” in Villanueva del Río y Minas.

The Rivera del Huéznar enters El Pedroso from the northeast, and runs from north to south throughout the eastern part of the term until it reaches the area where it is regulated by the reservoir of the same name, from where it emerges drawing the limit between El Pedroso and Constantina.

This site is the ruins of what was a great Roman city between the 1st and 4th centuries AD. C. The complex had all kinds of facilities: religious temples, a necropolis, hot springs, etc. Mulva-Munigua's success as an iron producer was such that for many years it was the administrative, judicial and religious center of the entire region.

It was declared an Asset of Cultural Interest in 1931.

Admission is completely free, and the opening hours are from 10:00 a.m. to 2:00 p.m., from Wednesday to Sunday.
The lake is located near the town center, a few meters from the Iron Bridge over the Rivera del Huéznar.

waypoints.name: Iron Bridge of Villanueva del Río y Minas
